violations.text: Egg rolls was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025
Establishment is lacking an allergy statement notifying customers of their obligation to notify staff if they have a food allergy. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

Garlic was observed held at 65 degrees Fahrenheit at the wok station. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC moved to refrigeration at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

Chicken fingers was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

(Chemical) were stored on the same shelf as spices. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. At time of inspection, noodles were observed cooling on a sheet rack out of refrigeration. Per interview with PIC, noodles were made 2 hours prior, temperature was observed at 67-71F. Noodles were relocated to walk-in cooler at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Uncovered containers of spices were observed in the dry storage hallway. Food items must be protected from contamination by being properly covered or stored in sealed containers. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

Knives were stored on an unclean surface (in between prep unit and table) between uses. Scoops were stored on an unclean surface (top of container) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

Wood is used in the construction of shelving in the dry storage area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the hoods have an accumulation of grease and soil residue. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2024
Menus are lacking an allergy statement notifying customers of their obligation to notify staff if they have a food allergy. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back of the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024
Food was observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler and dry storage area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Food was observed uncovered in the walk-in cooler and prep unit. Food must be covered to prevent contamination from the premises. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Wood is used in the construction of shelves in the dry storage area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024
